new_classes.definition_en: The French province of Champagne, serving as the designated geographical origin for grapes and wine production under the Champagne appellation.
A sparkling wine production process carried out exclusively in the French province of Champagne using grapes exclusively harvested in that province.

new_classes.label_en: grape harvested in the Champagne province
new_classes.label_de: in der Provinz Champagne geerntete Traube
new_classes.definition_en: A grape exclusively harvested within the French province of Champagne, constituting the sole permitted raw material for Champagne production.
A sparkling wine produced exclusively in the French province of Champagne from grapes exclusively harvested in that province, classified under CN code 2204 10 11.

coverage_explanation: The proposed axioms capture all three main distinguishing criteria from the legal text: (1) Champagne is a sparkling wine (subClassOf SparklingWine), (2) produced in the French province of Champagne (hasProductionRegion some ChampagneRegion), and (3) made exclusively from grapes harvested in that province (hasPart some ChampagneGrape, hasGrapeOrigin some ChampagneRegion, producedBy some ChampagneProductionProcess). A small gap remains: the exclusivity constraint ("exclusively" harvested and produced in Champagne) is expressed via existential (someValuesFrom) restrictions rather than a universal (allValuesFrom) constraint, which would be needed for full logical exclusivity. Full exclusivity modelling with allValuesFrom would risk over-constraining open-world reasoning, so conservative someValuesFrom is preferred, hence coverage_score of 0.9.
